<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Wavelets</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

Wavelets

Bases of $L^2(\mathbb{R})$ that see *where* a signal changes ŌĆö not just *which frequencies* it contains. Mallat's nested-subspace recipe, the Haar prototype, Daubechies' compactly supported family, the linear-time pyramid algorithm, and what happens when you replace JPEG's blocked DCT with a global wavelet basis.

blocks.html:
1. Multiresolution analysis

A Fourier basis spreads a localised event ŌĆö a cliff, a transient ŌĆö across infinitely many coefficients. Mallat's multiresolution analysis (MRA) builds a different basis, one organised by scale: at each dyadic resolution we keep a closed subspace $V_j\subset L^2(\mathbb{R})$ of "functions visible at scale $2^{-j}$", and we look at what extra detail $W_j=V_{j+1}\ominus V_j$ adds when we double the resolution.

The MRA axioms ŌĆö nesting, density, separation, dyadic dilation, and a scaling function $\phi$ whose integer translates orthonormally span $V_0$ ŌĆö together force $L^2(\mathbb{R})=\bigoplus_{j\in\mathbb{Z}} W_j$ and produce a single function $\psi$ (the wavelet) whose dilations and translations $\psi_{j,k}(x)=2^{j/2}\psi(2^j x-k)$ form an orthonormal basis of all of $L^2(\mathbb{R})$.

MRA tower
Slide $j$ to refine.

The picture above projects a smooth bump $f(x)=e^{-(x-0.5)^2/0.05}$ onto the Haar $V_j$: at $j=0$ the projection is one constant on $[0,1]$; each step $j\to j+1$ halves the cell width and the projection looks more like $f$. The detail subspace $W_j=V_{j+1}\ominus V_j$ is what got added. As $j\to\infty$ the projections converge in $L^2$ (and pointwise a.e. by Lebesgue differentiation) to $f$.

See also
  • Fourier transform on $\mathbb{R}$
  • LßĄ¢ spaces

Used in
Wavelets ┬Ę The Haar wavelet
</section>

2. The Haar wavelet

The simplest MRA: take $V_j$ to be all $L^2$ functions that are constant on each dyadic interval $[2^{-j}k,\,2^{-j}(k+1))$. The scaling function is $\phi=\chi_{[0,1)}$ ŌĆö its integer translates tile the line, orthonormally in $L^2$. The associated wavelet is the difference of two halves,

$$\psi(x) = \chi_{[0,1/2)}(x) - \chi_{[1/2,1)}(x).$$

Then $\{\psi_{j,k}\}_{j,k\in\mathbb{Z}}$ with $\psi_{j,k}(x)=2^{j/2}\psi(2^j x-k)$ is an orthonormal basis of $L^2(\mathbb{R})$ ŌĆö the Haar basis, discovered in 1909 a quarter-century before FourierŌĆōPlancherel even had a name.

Haar coefficient
Pick scale $j$ and translation $k$.

Each Haar coefficient $\langle f,\psi_{j,k}\rangle$ is a (rescaled) difference of two block averages: average of $f$ on the left half of the dyadic cell minus the average on the right half. So the Haar coefficient sees local change, while a Fourier coefficient $\hat f(n)$ sees global oscillation. The price the Haar basis pays: only one vanishing moment ($\int\psi=0$, but $\int x\psi\ne 0$), so it barely sees linear trends.

See also
  • Monotone and dominated convergence

Used in
Wavelets ┬Ę Daubechies wavelets
Wavelets ┬Ę Time-frequency localisation
</section>

3. Daubechies wavelets

The Haar wavelet is short but rough ŌĆö a step function. To get a wavelet that is both compactly supported and smooth, Ingrid Daubechies (1988) ran the MRA construction backwards: start from a finite filter $h_n$, ask for the refinement equation $\phi(x)=\sqrt 2\sum_n h_n\,\phi(2x-n)$ to produce a valid scaling function, and impose enough algebraic conditions on $h_n$ to force orthonormality plus $N$ vanishing moments.

The result is a family $\mathrm{db}N$ ($N=1,2,3,\ldots$) of compactly supported orthonormal wavelets with $\operatorname{supp}\psi=[0,2N-1]$ and $N$ vanishing moments. $\mathrm{db}1$ is Haar; $\mathrm{db}2$ is the first non-trivial member with the iconic asymmetric, fractal-looking graph below.

Daubechies family
Trade-off: smoothness vs. support.

The QMF condition $|m_0(\xi)|^2+|m_0(\xi+\tfrac12)|^2=1$ on the filter symbol $m_0(\xi)=\tfrac{1}{\sqrt 2}\sum_n h_n e^{-2\pi i n\xi}$ is what guarantees the integer translates of $\phi$ stay orthonormal under refinement. Daubechies's brilliant move: spectral-factor the polynomial $P(y)=\sum_{k=0}^{N-1}\binom{N-1+k}{k}y^k$ to extract a minimum-phase filter $h_n$ realising $N$ vanishing moments with the shortest possible support.

See also
  • Fourier transform on $\mathbb{R}$

Used in
Wavelets ┬Ę Discrete wavelet transform
</section>

4. Discrete wavelet transform

Mallat's pyramid algorithm computes wavelet coefficients faster than the FFT computes Fourier coefficients. The trick: nesting $V_j\subset V_{j+1}$ means the level-$j$ scaling coefficients $a_j$ are obtained from the level-$(j{+}1)$ scaling coefficients $a_{j+1}$ by convolution with a fixed low-pass filter $h$ then downsampling by $2$. Symmetrically, the level-$j$ wavelet (detail) coefficients $d_j$ come from convolving with the high-pass mirror filter $g$ and downsampling.

$$a_j[k] = \sum_n h[n-2k]\,a_{j+1}[n], \qquad d_j[k] = \sum_n g[n-2k]\,a_{j+1}[n].$$

Iterate on the low-pass branch only. After $\log_2 n$ levels you've replaced the original length-$n$ signal with one scalar (the global average) plus $n-1$ detail coefficients organised by scale. Total cost: $L\cdot n + L\cdot n/2 + L\cdot n/4 + \cdots = 2Ln = O(n)$ for an $L$-tap filter ŌĆö strictly faster than the FFT's $O(n\log n)$.

DWT pyramid (Haar)
Click "step" to recurse one level.
level 0 of 4

Inverse DWT mirrors analysis: upsample $a_j,d_j$ by inserting zeros, convolve with the dual reconstruction filters, and sum. For an orthonormal wavelet the reconstruction filters are the time-reversals of the analysis filters, so the inverse pyramid is also $O(n)$. Software libraries (PyWavelets, MATLAB Wavelet Toolbox) implement this in a hundred lines of straight loops.

Used in
Wavelets ┬Ę Applications
</section>

5. TimeŌĆōfrequency localisation

The Fourier transform $\hat f(\xi)$ tells you what frequencies are present in $f$ ŌĆö but every frequency coefficient depends on the entire signal, so a transient at $x_0$ is invisible to $|\hat f(\xi)|$ alone. The windowed Fourier (Gabor) transform fixes this by using a window of fixed width $\sigma$, but then high-frequency events get over-resolved in space and low-frequency events under-resolved.

Wavelets adapt: the atom $\psi_{j,k}$ has spatial width $\sim 2^{-j}$ and frequency width $\sim 2^j$, saturating the Heisenberg inequality $\Delta x\,\Delta\xi\gtrsim 1$. Cells are tall-and-thin at high frequency, short-and-wide at low frequency ŌĆö exactly the trade-off needed to track jumps and oscillations in the same basis.

Heisenberg tiling
Toggle Gabor vs. wavelet.

The vanishing-moment payoff is quantitative. If $f\in C^N$ in a neighbourhood of $2^{-j}k$ and $\psi$ has $N$ vanishing moments, Taylor expansion plus integration kills the first $N$ terms and leaves $|\langle f,\psi_{j,k}\rangle|=O(2^{-j(N+1/2)})$. So smooth signals concentrate their wavelet energy in a small number of large coefficients near singularities ŌĆö this is the engine behind every wavelet-based compression and denoising scheme.

See also
  • Fourier transform on $\mathbb{R}$

Used in
Wavelets ┬Ę Applications
</section>

6. Applications

Wavelets are not just a theoretical curiosity. Three vignettes:

Image compression (JPEG2000)

Legacy JPEG quantises the DCT of each $8\times 8$ tile independently ŌĆö at low bitrates, you see block boundaries because each tile rounds independently. JPEG2000 replaces the blocked DCT with a global CohenŌĆōDaubechiesŌĆōFeauveau (CDF) 9/7 biorthogonal DWT applied to the whole image, then quantises and entropy-codes the wavelet coefficients. Aggressive compression looks like overall smoothing, not mosaic artefacts.

Signal denoising (DonohoŌĆōJohnstone soft-thresholding)

Take a noisy signal $y=f+\varepsilon$, $\varepsilon\sim\mathcal{N}(0,\sigma^2 I)$. Apply DWT, soft-threshold each coefficient via $\hat c=\operatorname{sgn}(c)(|c|-\lambda)_+$ with $\lambda=\sigma\sqrt{2\log n}$, invert. Why it works: the wavelet transform of white Gaussian noise is again white Gaussian (orthonormal change of basis), spread thinly over all coefficients; smooth $f$ concentrates its energy in a few large coefficients. Thresholding kills the noise floor and keeps the spikes ŌĆö DonohoŌĆōJohnstone proved this estimator is asymptotically minimax over Besov balls.

Soft-threshold denoiser
Slide threshold; watch the reconstruction sharpen, then over-smooth.

Calder├│nŌĆōZygmund operators

If $T$ is a Calder├│nŌĆōZygmund operator (the Hilbert transform, the Riesz transforms, $-\Delta^{-1}$, and other singular-integral cousins) and $\psi$ has $N$ vanishing moments, BeylkinŌĆōCoifmanŌĆōRokhlin showed the matrix entries $\langle T\psi_{j,k},\psi_{j',k'}\rangle$ decay as $2^{-(N+1)|j-j'|}(1+|2^j k-2^{j'}k'|)^{-N-1}$. The matrix of $T$ in a wavelet basis is therefore sparse: only $O(n)$ entries are non-negligible. Applying $T$ to a signal in the wavelet basis costs $O(n)$ instead of $O(n^2)$ ŌĆö the engine behind fast multipole and adaptive PDE solvers.
</section>

Connections

Wavelets sit at the junction of harmonic analysis, signal processing, and PDE numerics. The frontier is partly geometric ŌĆö wavelets on graphs, manifolds, and groups (continuous wavelet transforms attached to Lie group representations) ŌĆö and partly statistical: optimal-rate adaptive estimation in Besov spaces, sparse recovery via $\ell^1$ relaxations, and the deeper connection between vanishing moments and approximation theory.

  • Harmonic analysis & Fourier ŌĆö Fourier transform ŌĆö wavelets refine the Fourier picture by adding spatial localisation; the QMF condition lives on the Fourier side.
  • Harmonic analysis & Fourier ŌĆö Heisenberg uncertainty ŌĆö every wavelet atom saturates the lower bound; Gabor and wavelet are two ways to tile $(\Delta x)\times(\Delta\xi)$ space.
  • Sobolev spaces ŌĆö $W^{k,p}$ and $H^s$ ŌĆö wavelet coefficient decay characterises Besov and Sobolev regularity by sequence-space norms.
  • Functional analysis ŌĆö orthonormal bases ŌĆö the wavelet expansion theorem is a Hilbert basis statement.
  • PDE ŌĆö adaptive wavelet methods solve elliptic problems with optimal $N\log N$ complexity by exploiting CZ-operator sparsity.

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/┬Ā/g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* ---- Widget 1: MRA tower ---- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("mra-svg");
  const slider = document.getElementById("mra-slider");
  const jLabel = document.getElementById("mra-j");
  const readout = document.getElementById("mra-readout");
  if(!svg || !slider) return;
  const W = 760, H = 240;
  const padL = 50, padR = 20, padT = 20, padB = 30;
  const xmin = 0, xmax = 1;
  function f(x){ return Math.exp(-Math.pow((x-0.5),2)/0.05); }
  function projectHaar(j){
    const N = 1 << j;
    const out = [];
    for(let k=0; k<N; k++){
      const a = k/N, b = (k+1)/N;
      const M = 64;
      let sum = 0;
      for(let i=0; i<M; i++){
        const x = a + (i+0.5)*(b-a)/M;
        sum += f(x);
      }
      out.push(sum/M);
    }
    return out;
  }
  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const j = +slider.value;
    jLabel.textContent = j;
    const xs = (x)=> padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin) * (W-padL-padR);
    const ys = (y)=> padT + (1 - y) * (H-padT-padB);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:H-padB,x2:W-padR,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:padT,x2:padL,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // smooth f(x)
    let d = "";
    for(let i=0;i<=200;i++){
      const x = xmin + i/200*(xmax-xmin);
      const y = f(x);
      d += (i===0?"M":"L")+xs(x)+" "+ys(y)+" ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1.4,"stroke-dasharray":"4 4"}));
    // projection: piecewise constant
    const proj = projectHaar(j);
    const N = proj.length;
    for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
      const x0 = k/N, x1 = (k+1)/N, val = proj[k];
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
        x: xs(x0), y: ys(val),
        width: xs(x1)-xs(x0), height: ys(0)-ys(val),
        fill:"var(--blue)", "fill-opacity":"0.18",
        stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":1.5
      }));
    }
    // labels
    const t = SVG("text",{x:padL+8,y:padT+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    t.textContent = "f (dashed) and its projection onto V_"+j+" (blue)";
    svg.appendChild(t);
    const t2 = SVG("text",{x:W-padR-8,y:H-padB+18,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"end"});
    t2.textContent = "x";
    svg.appendChild(t2);
    // readout: l2 error
    let err = 0;
    const M = 400;
    for(let i=0;i<M;i++){
      const x = (i+0.5)/M;
      const k = Math.min(N-1, Math.floor(x*N));
      const dy = f(x)-proj[k];
      err += dy*dy;
    }
    err = Math.sqrt(err/M);
    readout.textContent = "V_"+j+" has 2^"+j+" = "+N+" cells of width 2^-"+j+" = "+(1/N).toFixed(4)+
      "  ŌĆó  ŌĆ¢f ŌłÆ P_j fŌĆ¢Ōéé Ōēł "+err.toFixed(4);
  }
  slider.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* ---- Widget 2: Haar coefficient ---- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("haar-svg");
  const jSlide = document.getElementById("haar-jslider");
  const kSlide = document.getElementById("haar-kslider");
  const jLabel = document.getElementById("haar-j");
  const kLabel = document.getElementById("haar-k");
  const readout = document.getElementById("haar-readout");
  if(!svg) return;
  const W = 760, H = 240;
  const padL = 50, padR = 20, padT = 20, padB = 30;
  function f(x){
    if(x<0 || x>1) return 0;
    return 0.6 + 0.35*Math.sin(4*Math.PI*x) + (x>0.7 ? 0.4 : 0);
  }
  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const j = +jSlide.value, kMax = (1<<j) - 1;
    kSlide.max = kMax;
    if(+kSlide.value > kMax) kSlide.value = kMax;
    const k = +kSlide.value;
    jLabel.textContent = j;
    kLabel.textContent = k;
    const xs = (x)=> padL + x * (W-padL-padR);
    const ys = (y)=> padT + (1.4 - y)/2.6 * (H-padT-padB);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:ys(0),x2:W-padR,y2:ys(0),stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:padT,x2:padL,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // f(x)
    let d = "";
    for(let i=0;i<=300;i++){
      const x = i/300;
      const y = f(x);
      d += (i===0?"M":"L")+xs(x)+" "+ys(y)+" ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1.4}));
    // Žł_{j,k}: support [k/2^j, (k+1)/2^j], amplitude ┬▒2^{j/2}
    const a = k/(1<<j), b = (k+1)/(1<<j);
    const mid = (a+b)/2;
    const amp = Math.pow(2, j/2);
    // left half +amp
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
      x:xs(a), y:ys(amp), width:xs(mid)-xs(a), height:ys(0)-ys(amp),
      fill:"var(--yellow)","fill-opacity":"0.25",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.5
    }));
    // right half -amp
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
      x:xs(mid), y:ys(0), width:xs(b)-xs(mid), height:ys(0)-ys(-amp),
      fill:"var(--pink)","fill-opacity":"0.25",stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":1.5
    }));
    // compute coefficient
    const M = 400;
    let coef = 0;
    for(let i=0;i<M;i++){
      const x = a + (i+0.5)*(b-a)/M;
      const y = f(x);
      const psi = (x<mid? amp : -amp);
      coef += y*psi;
    }
    coef *= (b-a)/M;
    // labels
    const t = SVG("text",{x:padL+8,y:padT+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    t.textContent = "f(x) (gray) and Žł_{"+j+","+k+"} (yellow / pink)";
    svg.appendChild(t);
    readout.textContent = "support [" + a.toFixed(4) + ", " + b.toFixed(4) + "]   amplitude ┬▒2^(j/2) = ┬▒" +
      amp.toFixed(3) + "    Ō¤©f, Žł_{"+j+","+k+"}Ō¤® Ōēł " + coef.toFixed(4);
  }
  jSlide.addEventListener("input", draw);
  kSlide.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* ---- Widget 3: Daubechies family (cascade approximation) ---- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("db-svg");
  const buttons = document.querySelectorAll("#db-buttons button");
  const readout = document.getElementById("db-readout");
  if(!svg) return;
  const W = 760, H = 240;
  const padL = 50, padR = 20, padT = 20, padB = 30;
  // Daubechies low-pass filters (sqrt(2)-normalized so sum = sqrt(2))
  const filters = {
    1: [0.7071067811865475, 0.7071067811865475],
    2: [0.4829629131445341, 0.8365163037378079, 0.2241438680420134, -0.1294095225512604],
    3: [0.3326705529500825, 0.8068915093110924, 0.4598775021184914,
       -0.1350110200102546, -0.0854412738820267, 0.0352262918857095],
    4: [0.2303778133088964, 0.7148465705529154, 0.6308807679298587,
       -0.0279837694168599, -0.1870348117190931, 0.0308413818355607,
        0.0328830116668852, -0.0105974017850690]
  };
  function cascade(h, levels){
    // Generate phi by iterated cascade: phi_0 = box; phi_{k+1}(x) = sqrt(2) sum_n h[n] phi_k(2x - n)
    // Implemented on a fine grid using upsample-and-convolve.
    const L = h.length;
    let phi = [1];
    let dt = 1; // current sample spacing
    for(let it=0; it<levels; it++){
      // upsample phi by 2 (insert zeros between samples)
      const up = [];
      for(let i=0;i<phi.length;i++){ up.push(phi[i]); up.push(0); }
      // convolve with h and scale by sqrt(2)
      const out = new Array(up.length + L - 1).fill(0);
      for(let i=0;i<up.length;i++){
        for(let j=0;j<L;j++){
          out[i+j] += up[i]*h[j]*Math.SQRT2;
        }
      }
      phi = out;
      dt = dt/2;
    }
    return {phi, dt, support: (L-1)};
  }
  function psiFromPhi(h, phi){
    // psi(x) = sqrt(2) sum_n g[n] phi(2x - n), g[n] = (-1)^n h[L-1-n]
    const L = h.length;
    const g = h.map((_,n)=> ((n%2===0)?1:-1)*h[L-1-n]);
    // upsample phi by 2
    const up = [];
    for(let i=0;i<phi.length;i++){ up.push(phi[i]); up.push(0); }
    const out = new Array(up.length + L - 1).fill(0);
    for(let i=0;i<up.length;i++){
      for(let j=0;j<L;j++){
        out[i+j] += up[i]*g[j]*Math.SQRT2;
      }
    }
    return out;
  }
  function draw(N){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const h = filters[N];
    const levels = (N<=2)? 8 : (N===3? 7 : 6);
    const {phi, dt, support} = cascade(h, levels);
    const psi = psiFromPhi(h, phi);
    // Determine plot extent: x from 0 to support
    const xmax = support;
    let yMin = Infinity, yMax = -Infinity;
    for(const v of phi){ if(v<yMin)yMin=v; if(v>yMax)yMax=v; }
    for(const v of psi){ if(v<yMin)yMin=v; if(v>yMax)yMax=v; }
    yMin -= 0.1; yMax += 0.1;
    const xs = (x)=> padL + x/xmax*(W-padL-padR);
    const ys = (y)=> padT + (yMax - y)/(yMax-yMin)*(H-padT-padB);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:ys(0),x2:W-padR,y2:ys(0),stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:padT,x2:padL,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // phi
    let dPhi = "";
    for(let i=0;i<phi.length;i++){
      const x = i*dt;
      if(x>xmax) break;
      dPhi += (i===0?"M":"L")+xs(x)+" "+ys(phi[i])+" ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dPhi,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":1.6}));
    // psi
    let dPsi = "";
    for(let i=0;i<psi.length;i++){
      const x = i*dt;
      if(x>xmax) break;
      dPsi += (i===0?"M":"L")+xs(x)+" "+ys(psi[i])+" ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dPsi,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.6}));
    // legend
    const lg = SVG("text",{x:padL+8,y:padT+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    lg.textContent = "scaling Žå"; svg.appendChild(lg);
    const lg2 = SVG("text",{x:padL+90,y:padT+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    lg2.textContent = "wavelet Žł"; svg.appendChild(lg2);
    // x label
    const xl = SVG("text",{x:W-padR,y:H-padB+18,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"end"});
    xl.textContent = "x Ōłł [0, "+support+"]"; svg.appendChild(xl);
    // readout
    readout.textContent = "db"+N+":  filter taps = "+(2*N)+"   support [0, "+(2*N-1)+"]   vanishing moments = "+N+
      "   smoothness Ōēł "+(N===1? "0 (step)": (N===2? "0.55": (N===3? "1.09": "1.62")));
  }
  buttons.forEach(b=>{
    b.addEventListener("click", ()=>{
      buttons.forEach(x=>x.classList.remove("active"));
      b.classList.add("active");
      draw(+b.dataset.db);
    });
  });
  draw(1);
})();
</script>

<script>
/* ---- Widget 4: DWT pyramid ---- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("dwt-svg");
  const stepBtn = document.getElementById("dwt-step");
  const resetBtn = document.getElementById("dwt-reset");
  const levelLabel = document.getElementById("dwt-level");
  const readout = document.getElementById("dwt-readout");
  if(!svg) return;
  const W = 760, H = 280;
  const initial = [4, 6, 10, 12, 8, 6, 5, 5, 3, 2, 6, 8, 10, 9, 4, 1];
  const maxLevels = Math.log2(initial.length);
  let approx = initial.slice();
  let history = [{level:0, a: initial.slice(), d: []}];
  function haarStep(a){
    const n = a.length;
    const out_a = new Array(n/2);
    const out_d = new Array(n/2);
    for(let k=0;k<n/2;k++){
      out_a[k] = (a[2*k] + a[2*k+1])/Math.SQRT2;
      out_d[k] = (a[2*k] - a[2*k+1])/Math.SQRT2;
    }
    return {a: out_a, d: out_d};
  }
  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const padL = 30, padR = 30, padT = 30, padB = 20;
    const rowH = 40;
    let allMax = 0;
    history.forEach(row=>{
      row.a.forEach(v=>{ if(Math.abs(v)>allMax) allMax = Math.abs(v); });
      row.d.forEach(v=>{ if(Math.abs(v)>allMax) allMax = Math.abs(v); });
    });
    if(allMax === 0) allMax = 1;
    history.forEach((row, idx)=>{
      const y = padT + idx*rowH;
      // approx coefficients
      const aN = row.a.length;
      const cellW = (W - padL - padR) / initial.length;
      const aWidth = cellW * (1<<idx);
      for(let i=0;i<aN;i++){
        const x = padL + i*aWidth;
        const v = row.a[i];
        const intensity = Math.min(1, Math.abs(v)/allMax);
        svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
          x, y, width: aWidth, height: rowH-6,
          fill: "var(--cyan)", "fill-opacity": (0.15 + 0.5*intensity).toFixed(3),
          stroke: "var(--cyan)", "stroke-width": 1
        }));
        const t = SVG("text",{x: x + aWidth/2, y: y + rowH/2 + 1,
          "text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11, fill:"var(--ink)"});
        t.textContent = v.toFixed(2);
        svg.appendChild(t);
      }
      // detail coefficients (right side, same width as approx)
      if(row.d.length){
        const dN = row.d.length;
        const dWidth = aWidth;
        for(let i=0;i<dN;i++){
          const x = padL + (aN)*aWidth + i*dWidth;
          const v = row.d[i];
          const intensity = Math.min(1, Math.abs(v)/allMax);
          svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
            x, y, width: dWidth, height: rowH-6,
            fill: "var(--yellow)", "fill-opacity": (0.15 + 0.5*intensity).toFixed(3),
            stroke: "var(--yellow)", "stroke-width": 1
          }));
          const t = SVG("text",{x: x + dWidth/2, y: y + rowH/2 + 1,
            "text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11, fill:"var(--ink)"});
          t.textContent = v.toFixed(2);
          svg.appendChild(t);
        }
      }
      // row label
      const lbl = SVG("text",{x: 4, y: y + rowH/2 + 1, "font-size": 11, fill: "var(--mute)"});
      lbl.textContent = "L"+idx;
      svg.appendChild(lbl);
    });
    // header
    const h1 = SVG("text",{x: padL+5, y: padT-8, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    h1.textContent = "approx (low-pass)"; svg.appendChild(h1);
    const h2 = SVG("text",{x: W-padR-100, y: padT-8, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    h2.textContent = "detail (high-pass)"; svg.appendChild(h2);
  }
  function step(){
    const last = history[history.length-1];
    if(last.a.length <= 1) return;
    const {a, d} = haarStep(last.a);
    history.push({level: last.level+1, a, d});
    levelLabel.textContent = "level "+(history.length-1)+" of "+maxLevels;
    draw();
    const energy = (last.a.reduce((s,v)=>s+v*v,0)).toFixed(2);
    const newEnergy = (a.reduce((s,v)=>s+v*v,0) + d.reduce((s,v)=>s+v*v,0)).toFixed(2);
    readout.textContent = "After step "+history.length+":  ŌĆ¢aŌĆ¢┬▓ + ŌĆ¢dŌĆ¢┬▓ = "+newEnergy+"  (was ŌĆ¢prevŌĆ¢┬▓ = "+energy+", energy preserved)";
  }
  function reset(){
    history = [{level:0, a: initial.slice(), d: []}];
    levelLabel.textContent = "level 0 of "+maxLevels;
    readout.textContent = "Initial signal length "+initial.length+".  Click step to apply Haar low/high-pass + downsample by 2.";
    draw();
  }
  stepBtn.addEventListener("click", step);
  resetBtn.addEventListener("click", reset);
  reset();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* ---- Widget 5: Heisenberg tiling ---- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("tile-svg");
  const buttons = document.querySelectorAll("#tile-mode button");
  const readout = document.getElementById("tile-readout");
  if(!svg) return;
  const W = 760, H = 320;
  const padL = 60, padR = 20, padT = 20, padB = 40;
  function draw(mode){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const xMin = 0, xMax = 1;
    const fMin = 0, fMax = 64;
    const xs = (x)=> padL + (x-xMin)/(xMax-xMin)*(W-padL-padR);
    const ys = (f)=> padT + (1 - (f-fMin)/(fMax-fMin))*(H-padT-padB);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:H-padB,x2:W-padR,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:padT,x2:padL,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    const xlbl = SVG("text",{x: (W-padR+padL)/2, y: H-padB+30, "font-size":12, "text-anchor":"middle", fill:"var(--mute)"});
    xlbl.textContent = "time x"; svg.appendChild(xlbl);
    const ylbl = SVG("text",{x: 12, y: (H-padB+padT)/2, "font-size":12, "text-anchor":"middle", fill:"var(--mute)",
      transform:"rotate(-90 12 "+((H-padB+padT)/2)+")"});
    ylbl.textContent = "frequency ╬Š"; svg.appendChild(ylbl);
    if(mode === "wavelet"){
      // Dyadic tiles: at scale j there are 2^j tiles each of x-width 2^-j and frequency band [2^j, 2^{j+1}]
      const colors = ["var(--blue)","var(--cyan)","var(--green)","var(--yellow)","var(--pink)","var(--violet)"];
      for(let j=0; j<6; j++){
        const fLo = (1<<j) - 1;
        const fHi = (1<<(j+1)) - 1;
        const N = 1 << j;
        for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
          const x0 = k/N, x1 = (k+1)/N;
          svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
            x: xs(x0), y: ys(fHi),
            width: xs(x1)-xs(x0), height: ys(fLo)-ys(fHi),
            fill: colors[j], "fill-opacity":"0.18",
            stroke: colors[j], "stroke-width":1
          }));
        }
      }
      readout.textContent = "Wavelet tiling: at scale j, 2^j atoms of width 2^-j and bandwidth ~2^j. Tall-thin at high frequency, wide-short at low frequency.";
    } else {
      // Gabor: uniform grid, e.g. 8 time x 8 freq cells
      const NX = 8, NF = 8;
      const dxC = (xMax-xMin)/NX, dfC = (fMax-fMin)/NF;
      for(let i=0;i<NX;i++){
        for(let k=0;k<NF;k++){
          const x0 = xMin + i*dxC, x1 = x0+dxC;
          const f0 = fMin + k*dfC, f1 = f0+dfC;
          svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
            x: xs(x0), y: ys(f1),
            width: xs(x1)-xs(x0), height: ys(f0)-ys(f1),
            fill: "var(--blue)", "fill-opacity":"0.15",
            stroke: "var(--blue)", "stroke-width":1
          }));
        }
      }
      readout.textContent = "Gabor (windowed Fourier) tiling: uniform cells of fixed window width Žā. Same shape at every frequency ŌĆö over-resolves slow events, under-resolves fast ones.";
    }
  }
  buttons.forEach(b=>{
    b.addEventListener("click", ()=>{
      buttons.forEach(x=>x.classList.remove("active"));
      b.classList.add("active");
      draw(b.dataset.mode);
    });
  });
  draw("wavelet");
})();
</script>

<script>
/* ---- Widget 6: Soft-threshold denoiser ---- */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("den-svg");
  const sigSlide = document.getElementById("den-sigma");
  const lamSlide = document.getElementById("den-lambda");
  const sLabel = document.getElementById("den-s");
  const lLabel = document.getElementById("den-l");
  const readout = document.getElementById("den-readout");
  if(!svg) return;
  const W = 760, H = 280;
  const padL = 50, padR = 20, padT = 20, padB = 30;
  const N = 256;
  // Truth signal: piecewise smooth + jump
  const truth = [];
  for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
    const x = i/N;
    let v = Math.sin(3*Math.PI*x) * 0.6;
    if(x>0.55) v += 0.7;
    truth.push(v);
  }
  // Pseudo-random Gaussian via Box-Muller, deterministic seed
  const noise = [];
  let s = 12345;
  function rand(){ s = (s*1103515245 + 12345) & 0x7fffffff; return s / 0x7fffffff; }
  for(let i=0;i<N;i+=2){
    const u1 = Math.max(rand(), 1e-9), u2 = rand();
    const r = Math.sqrt(-2*Math.log(u1)), th = 2*Math.PI*u2;
    noise.push(r*Math.cos(th));
    noise.push(r*Math.sin(th));
  }
  function fullHaarDWT(a){
    const coefs = []; // levels of detail
    let cur = a.slice();
    while(cur.length > 1){
      const n = cur.length;
      const A = new Array(n/2), D = new Array(n/2);
      for(let k=0;k<n/2;k++){
        A[k] = (cur[2*k]+cur[2*k+1])/Math.SQRT2;
        D[k] = (cur[2*k]-cur[2*k+1])/Math.SQRT2;
      }
      coefs.unshift(D); // store details from coarse to fine
      cur = A;
    }
    return {a: cur, details: coefs};
  }
  function inverseHaarDWT(a, details){
    let cur = a.slice();
    for(const D of details){
      const n = cur.length;
      const out = new Array(2*n);
      for(let k=0;k<n;k++){
        out[2*k] = (cur[k]+D[k])/Math.SQRT2;
        out[2*k+1] = (cur[k]-D[k])/Math.SQRT2;
      }
      cur = out;
    }
    return cur;
  }
  function softThreshold(c, lam){
    if(c > lam) return c - lam;
    if(c < -lam) return c + lam;
    return 0;
  }
  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const sigma = (+sigSlide.value)/100;
    const lam = (+lamSlide.value)/100;
    sLabel.textContent = sigma.toFixed(2);
    lLabel.textContent = lam.toFixed(2);
    // Build noisy signal
    const noisy = truth.map((v,i)=> v + sigma*noise[i]);
    // DWT
    const {a, details} = fullHaarDWT(noisy);
    // Threshold all detail levels
    const dThresh = details.map(D => D.map(c => softThreshold(c, lam)));
    const recon = inverseHaarDWT(a, dThresh);
    // RMS error
    let mseN = 0, mseR = 0;
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      mseN += (noisy[i]-truth[i])**2;
      mseR += (recon[i]-truth[i])**2;
    }
    mseN = Math.sqrt(mseN/N); mseR = Math.sqrt(mseR/N);
    // Plot
    const xs = (i)=> padL + i/(N-1)*(W-padL-padR);
    const ys = (y)=> padT + (1.5 - y)/3 * (H-padT-padB);
    // axis at zero
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:ys(0),x2:W-padR,y2:ys(0),stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    function plot(arr, color, width, opacity){
      let d = "";
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        d += (i===0?"M":"L")+xs(i)+" "+ys(arr[i])+" ";
      }
      svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:color, "stroke-width":width, "stroke-opacity":opacity}));
    }
    plot(noisy, "var(--mute)", 1, "0.6");
    plot(truth, "var(--blue)", 1.6, "1");
    plot(recon, "var(--yellow)", 1.6, "1");
    // Legend
    const lg = SVG("text",{x:padL+8,y:padT+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lg.textContent = "noisy"; svg.appendChild(lg);
    const lg2 = SVG("text",{x:padL+70,y:padT+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--blue)"});
    lg2.textContent = "truth"; svg.appendChild(lg2);
    const lg3 = SVG("text",{x:padL+130,y:padT+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    lg3.textContent = "denoised"; svg.appendChild(lg3);
    const universal = sigma * Math.sqrt(2*Math.log(N));
    readout.textContent = "RMS noisy = "+mseN.toFixed(3)+"   RMS denoised = "+mseR.toFixed(3)+
      "   universal threshold ŽāŌłÜ(2 log N) = "+universal.toFixed(3)+
      ((Math.abs(lam-universal)<0.04)? "  ŌåÉ near optimum" : "");
  }
  sigSlide.addEventListener("input", draw);
  lamSlide.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>
